title: update c/common
body:
Update containers common to the latest HEAD.  Some bug fixes in libimage
forced us to have a clearer separation between ordinary images and
manifest lists.  Hence, when looking up manifest lists without recursing
into any of their instances, we need to use `LookupManifestList()`.

Also account for some other changes in c/common (e.g., the changed order
in the security labels).

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@rhatdan will tackle the remaining bits here tomorrow.
Now including https://github.com/containers/common/pull/564 which should make the system tests pass.
Sigh...yet another regression in c/common. This time, evaluating the runtime in containers.conf.
Now vendored in the HEAD from Buildah to get the bud tests to pass.
@vrothberg please download this and save as test/buildah-bud/buildah-tests.diff  :  https://paste.centos.org/view/80ce0f8e

@vrothberg, I'm so sorry, please use [this instead](https://paste.centos.org/view/344362bd).

There's another error worth investigating, in [system tests](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6246973066969088/html/sys-podman-fedora-33-root-host.log.html#t--00030). I vaguely recall that this one is expected due to an error-message change in the new storage lib, do you remember the details?

And, in my own testing, it looks like the `bud with bad --tag` test is going to fail (that was added 2021-05-11 and it fails under podman). I suggest, to get things going quickly, adding:
```
skip "FIXME FIXME FIXME" "bud with bad --tag"
```
to `test/buildah-bud/apply-podman-deltas`. (It can probably be fixed via an `errmsg` directive instead, but we can work out the details later)

Thank you, Ed!

> There's another error worth investigating, in system tests. I vaguely recall that this one is expected due to an error-message change in the new storage lib, do you remember the details?
> "[...] can't talk to a V1 docker registry"

This error looks like a red herring. Maybe a temporary fart on Quay?
